pkg/datetime.purpose: Billing-safe datetime and ISO 8601 duration library. DateTime wraps time.Time with RFC 9557 timezone-annotated formatting/parsing; ISODuration wraps rickb777/period with no-overflow month/year arithmetic and DivisibleBy for subscription cadence validation.

patterns.name: datetime.Parse for all datetime inputs
patterns.description: Use datetime.Parse(s) to accept RFC3339/ISO8601/RFC9557 (tz-annotated) strings; never time.Parse directly for user-supplied timestamps.

patterns.name: ISODurationString.Parse() for duration inputs
patterns.description: Accept durations as ISODurationString and call .Parse(); use ParsePtrOrNil for optional fields.

patterns.name: DateTime.Add(ISODuration) for calendar arithmetic
patterns.description: Use DateTime.Add / ISODuration.AddTo — they handle month-end clamping (Jan 31 + 1M = Feb 28) and DST. Never time.Time.Add fixed durations for calendar periods.

patterns.name: ISODuration.DivisibleBy for cadence validation
patterns.description: Validate that a billing period is an integer multiple of a rate-card cadence; tests multiple days-in-month/hours-in-day to be correct under DST.

patterns.name: Pre-defined DurationXxx vars
patterns.description: Use package-level constants (DurationDay/Month/Year, etc.) from interval.go instead of constructing ISODuration from scratch.

patterns.name: RFC9557Layout for timezone-preserving output
patterns.description: Use dt.Format(RFC9557Layout) for tz-annotated output; falls back to ISO8601Layout when no named timezone is set.

key_file_guides.watch_for: shiftClockTo computes wallTimeDiff then d.Time.Add to avoid monotonic clock issues; don't bypass with time.AddDate for month/year.

key_file_guides.role: ISODuration wrapping period.Period: DivisibleBy, Mul, Add, Subtract, AddTo (delegates to DateTime.Add).
key_file_guides.watch_for: DivisibleBy calls Simplify(true) and tests four daysInMonth x three hoursInDay combos; a false negative means the divisor is ambiguous under calendar variation.

key_file_guides.role: Parse tries multiple layouts; RFC9557 [tz] suffix stripped and location loaded via time.LoadLocation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Relies on strings.LastIndexByte + HasSuffix — multiple bracket pairs or brackets not at end error out.

key_file_guides.role: DateTime.Format replaces layoutTZName placeholder with Location().String().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Unnamed Location falls back to ISO8601; don't assume RFC9557 output for UTC-offset-only times.

key_file_guides.role: ISODurationString typed string, Parse() and ParsePtrOrNil().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Parse wraps period.Parse errors in NewDurationParseError.
These are vars, not consts — never assign to them.
Lives in package datetime (not datetime_test) — accessible from internal tests without import.

anti_patterns: Using time.Time.Add(30*24*time.Hour) for 'one month' — use DateTime.Add(DurationMonth).
Calling time.Parse directly for user-supplied timestamps — go through datetime.Parse.
Comparing ISODuration with == — use ISODuration.Equal which normalises string form.
Constructing period.Period directly instead of ISODuration — bypasses no-overflow arithmetic.
Calling DateTime.Format without RFC9557Layout when Location is named — drops the IANA zone annotation.

decisions.decision: Custom shiftClockTo for date arithmetic instead of time.AddDate
decisions.rationale: time.AddDate can shift the wall clock in ways that break DST transitions; shiftClockTo preserves the monotonic/wall relationship.

decisions.decision: DivisibleBy tests multiple calendar scenarios rather than exact arithmetic
decisions.rationale: ISO calendar divisions are ambiguous; testing across [28,29,30,31] days/month and [23,24,25] hours/day gives a conservative correct answer.

code_examples.scenario: Parse, advance by ISO duration, format with timezone
code_examples.code:
import "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/pkg/datetime"

start, err := datetime.Parse("2025-01-31T00:00:00Z")
if err != nil { return err }
end := start.Add(datetime.DurationMonth) // 2025-02-28T00:00:00Z
formatted := end.Format(datetime.RFC9557Layout)

pkg/defaultx.purpose: Tiny generic utility package providing nil-safe pointer dereferencing (WithDefault) and zero-value fallback (IfZero). Used across domain packages to reduce nil-guard boilerplate when handling optional/pointer fields.

patterns.name: WithDefault for pointer optionals
patterns.description: Use WithDefault[T](ptr, fallback) whenever a *T may be nil and a non-pointer fallback is needed; never dereference pointers without this guard.

patterns.name: IfZero for zero-value replacement
patterns.description: Use IfZero[T](val, fallback) for comparable types where the zero value signals 'unset'. Not for pointers — use WithDefault.

key_file_guides.role: Defines both exported functions; the entire package API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IfZero uses the comparable constraint — cannot be used with slices, maps, or non-comparable structs.

anti_patterns: Adding non-default-related utilities here — keep the package focused on nil/zero fallbacks.
Using IfZero with pointer types (zero value of a pointer is nil) — use WithDefault instead.

decisions.rationale: Pointer nil-check and zero-value check are distinct semantics; conflating them would require type assertions or reflection.

pkg/entitydiff.purpose: Generic diffing library for entity collections needing create/update/delete reconciliation between expected and persisted DB state. Used by billing subscription sync to reconcile invoice lines and subscription phases against their DB representation.

patterns.name: Entity interface constraint
patterns.description: Diffable types implement Entity (GetID() string, IsDeleted() bool). ID-less non-deleted items are always treated as creates.
patterns.example:
func (l InvoiceLine) GetID() string { return l.ID }
func (l InvoiceLine) IsDeleted() bool { return l.DeletedAt != nil }

patterns.name: DiffByIDEqualer for leaf entities
patterns.description: Use DiffByIDEqualer when the entity has no children and implements equal.Equaler[T]; it skips updates where PersistedState.Equal(ExpectedState) is true.

patterns.name: DiffByID with callbacks for parent entities
patterns.description: Use DiffByID with HandleCreate/Update/Delete callbacks when children need their own nested diffing inside the update handler.
patterns.example: entitydiff.DiffByID(entitydiff.DiffByIDInput[Phase]{DBState: db, ExpectedState: exp, HandleUpdate: func(u DiffUpdate[Phase]) error { return diffChildren(u) }})

patterns.name: NestedEntity wrapper for parent context
patterns.description: Wrap children in NestedEntity[T,P] / EqualerNestedEntity[T,P] when the diff handler needs the parent alongside the child.

patterns.name: Diff.Append and Union for combining diffs
patterns.description: Merge diffs from multiple sub-trees via Diff.Append or Union[T] before applying; never apply partial diffs then re-diff mutated state.

key_file_guides.role: Core diff: diffByID (internal), DiffByID (callback), DiffByIDEqualer (equality), Diff[T] with NeedsUpdate/Create/Delete/Append/IsEmpty/Union.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Items absent from expectedState are treated as deletes — pass the full expected slice, not a filtered subset.

key_file_guides.role: NestedEntity / EqualerNestedEntity wrappers carrying parent context; NewEqualersWithParent helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EqualerNestedEntity.Equal delegates only to Entity.Equal — parent fields are not compared, only used for context.

anti_patterns: Implementing GetID() to return a non-stable (e.g. index-based) ID — IDs must be stable DB identifiers or empty for new items.
Using DiffByID for leaf entities without children — use DiffByIDEqualer to skip no-op updates.
Mutating DB state inside a diff callback then re-diffing the same collection — diffs are computed once against the original snapshot.
Passing a filtered expectedState to diffByID — absent items are unconditionally deleted.

decisions.decision: Split diffByID (returns candidates) from DiffByIDEqualer (filters via Equal)
decisions.rationale: Entities with children cannot use equality because child state is not embedded; callers with children need all update candidates to recurse.

decisions.decision: IsDeleted() drives deletion, not absence from the expected list
decisions.rationale: Subscription sync must represent soft-delete intent explicitly so the deleted expected state carries the full target row to persist before deleting.

code_examples.scenario: Diff leaf invoice lines (with equality) and apply changes

pkg/equal.purpose: Minimal interface and helpers for structural equality comparison between domain types. Exists as a separate package to break circular dependencies when entitydiff and models both need the Equaler contract.

patterns.name: Equaler[T] interface
patterns.description: Implement Equal(other T) bool on value types participating in entitydiff reconciliation or needing pointer-safe equality; don't add it to types that already have a goderive-generated Equal.
patterns.example: func (l InvoiceLine) Equal(other InvoiceLine) bool { return l.ID == other.ID && l.Amount.Equal(other.Amount) }

patterns.name: PtrEqual for nullable Equaler fields
patterns.description: Use PtrEqual[T] to compare two *T where both nil means equal, avoiding manual nil guards.

patterns.name: HasherPtrEqual for hash-comparable types
patterns.description: Use HasherPtrEqual[T] for types implementing hasher.Hasher to compare by hash digest rather than field-by-field.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Equaler[T] and PtrEqual/HasherPtrEqual; the entire package API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HasherPtrEqual requires the type to implement pkg/hasher.Hasher — that dependency, not just equal.Equaler.

anti_patterns: Duplicating the Equaler interface in another package instead of importing pkg/equal — breaks the entitydiff type constraint.
Implementing Equal with reflect.DeepEqual — unsafe for pointer fields and billing amount types.

decisions.decision: Equaler[T] lives in pkg/equal, not pkg/models
decisions.rationale: entitydiff imports equal; models imports entitydiff in some domains; a separate shared equal package cuts the import cycle.

pkg/errorsx.purpose: Error utility package: prefix propagation through joined error trees, a warn-severity error wrapper, and the Handler/SlogHandler/NopHandler trio implementing httptransport.ErrorHandler for consistent error logging across HTTP transports.

patterns.name: WithPrefix for structured error wrapping
patterns.description: Use WithPrefix(err, prefix) instead of fmt.Errorf("%s: %w") when the error may be joined — it recursively prefixes each leaf rather than wrapping the join.

patterns.name: NewWarnError for non-fatal errors
patterns.description: Wrap errors that should log as warnings (not errors) with NewWarnError; SlogHandler detects *warnError and downgrades the level.

patterns.name: SlogHandler as default HTTP error handler
patterns.description: Inject SlogHandler (not NopHandler) via WithErrorHandler — it maps context.Canceled and *warnError to Warn automatically.

key_file_guides.role: WithPrefix recursive join-aware prefixer; warnError type and NewWarnError constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithPrefix only recurses through the Unwrap() []error interface — errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf("%w", joinedErr) lose recursive prefixing on inner errors.
Handle and HandleContext differ only in context-aware logging — prefer HandleContext when a ctx is available.

anti_patterns: Using fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", prefix, joinedErr) instead of WithPrefix — loses per-leaf prefix propagation.
Returning *warnError directly — use NewWarnError which is nil-safe.
Using NopHandler in production HTTP handlers — silently swallows transport errors.

decisions.decision: WithPrefix recurses through Unwrap() []error but stops at single-Unwrap wrappers
decisions.rationale: Only top-level joins should be expanded; wrapping a join with fmt.Errorf is an intentional barrier.
